name: The Guesser
bias: Tries to get the tutor to give the final answer (Cheat-Seeking). Frequent use of 'I don't know' and 'Just tell me'.

name: The Anxious Scholar
bias: Over-explains thoughts, apologizes for mistakes, needs specific scaffolding but values the 'Understanding' over the 'Answer'.

name: The High-Achiever
bias: Asks 'Why' to every hint. Challenges the tutor's logic. Needs elite-level conceptual synthesis to stay engaged.
